name: South Yorkshire (United Kingdom, de facto)
description: An unofficial design: a red-and-white chevronny (zig-zag) field overlaid with black triangles at the hoist and fly and a central black lozenge, each bearing a white rose of York — the central rose whole, those at the ends halved.
history: An unofficial flag for the South Yorkshire metropolitan county, which has no Flag Institute–registered county flag of its own. The design draws on the white rose tradition of the historic county of Yorkshire; the stylised letters 'SY' form the stem of the central rose.

features.role: black triangles at the hoist and fly, each charged with half of a white rose
central black lozenge (rhombus) charged with the full white rose
white roses of York — one whole at the centre, two halves at the ends
